name: Crawford County Trust And Savings Bank
description_short: Crawford County Trust and Savings Bank is a community bank founded in 1927, offering personal and business banking services with a focus on local relationship banking in Denison, Iowa.
description_long: Crawford County Trust and Savings Bank traces its origins to 1927 and maintains its original name as a point of pride in community continuity. The bank emphasizes personal service and relationship-based banking, positioning itself as an alternative to larger institutional banks where customers are known by name rather than treated as transactions or quotas. Operating primarily in Crawford County, Iowa, the institution has maintained a community-focused operational model for nearly a century. The bank offers a comprehensive range of personal and business banking products including checking accounts, savings accounts, credit cards, mortgages, agricultural loans, and digital banking services. They provide access to 55,000 surcharge-free ATMs worldwide through the Allpoint ATM Network partnership. The institution maintains physical branch locations in Denison, Iowa, and supports both traditional in-person banking and digital/mobile banking channels with continual platform improvements. Crawford County Trust and Savings Bank distinguishes itself through deep community integration and local commitment. Beyond basic financial services, the bank actively sponsors and participates in community initiatives including After Prom, Relay-for-Life, 4-H Livestock Sale, Arts Council, FFA scholarships, and Donna Reed Scholarships. Many employees take leadership roles in these community programs, reflecting organizational values beyond profit maximization. The bank is FDIC-insured, providing federal deposit protection. As a small, regional community bank, Crawford County Trust and Savings Bank is best suited for customers prioritizing personal relationships and local community impact over extensive branch networks or cutting-edge digital features. The institution's strength lies in understanding local business needs and maintaining accessible personal banking relationships. However, customers seeking nationwide branch access, advanced fintech capabilities, or highly competitive rates may find limitations compared to larger regional or national institutions.
services: Digital banking and online account management
ATM access through Allpoint Network (55,000 locations)
Money management tools and financial guidance
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Access to 55,000 surcharge-free ATMs worldwide through Allpoint Network
Founded in 1927 with nearly 100 years of continuous operation and community presence
Personalized banking with relationship-based service model rather than transaction-focused
Strong community investment with sponsorships of local youth programs, scholarships, and civic organizations
Offers agricultural loans, addressing farming and rural business needs
Digital banking improvements with mobile and online banking platforms
Employees actively serve in leadership roles for community programs
cons: Limited physical branch presence (only mentioned Denison, IA location) restricts geographic accessibility
Website content provides no information on interest rates, fees, or product pricing
No mention of competitive features like high-yield savings or premium account tiers
Limited details on digital banking capabilities compared to major national banks
Small institution may lack resources for 24/7 customer support or advanced financial services
best_for: Crawford County, Iowa residents seeking relationship-based personal banking
Local small businesses and agricultural operations needing community bank partnerships
Customers prioritizing community impact and local economic support over national convenience
Individuals valuing personal service and decision-making by local loan officers
diagnosis: Crawford County Trust and Savings Bank is best for Crawford County, Iowa residents and local business owners seeking personalized, relationship-based banking from a community-committed institution. The primary caveat is limited geographic reach and branch presence outside Denison, Iowa, making it impractical for customers requiring multi-state access or those relocating outside the service area.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Crawford County Trust and Savings Bank in Denison, IA founded 1927, offers personal and business banking with local focus.
fdic_official_name: Crawford County Trust and Savings Bank
